assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files contain: Repository (e.g., 'trustify-backend'), Target Branch ('main'), Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements. Task 1 has Files to Create and Files to Modify. Task 2 has Files to Modify. Task 3 has Files to Create and Files to Modify. Task 4 has Files to Modify. Task 5 has Files to Create and Files to Modify.

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: There are exactly 5 task files: task-1-add-advisory-severity-summary-model.md, task-2-add-severity-aggregation-service-method.md, task-3-add-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-4-add-cache-invalidation-for-advisory-summaries.md, task-5-add-integration-tests-for-advisory-summary-endpoint.md. 5 is between 3 and 10 inclusive.
All 5 tasks have '## Repository' section with value 'trustify-backend'. Task 1 line 4, Task 2 line 4, Task 3 line 4, Task 4 line 4, Task 5 line 4.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1: no dependencies. Task 2: depends on Task 1. Task 3: depends on Task 2. Task 4: depends on Task 3. Task 5: depends on Task 3. This forms a linear chain 1->2->3 with 4 and 5 both depending on 3, which is a valid DAG with no cycles.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 creates 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs' (plausible, sibling to existing summary.rs and details.rs) and modifies 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs' (exists in tree). Task 2 modifies 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs' (exists in tree). Task 3 creates 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs' (plausible, sibling to existing get.rs and list.rs) and modifies 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs' (exists in tree). Task 4 modifies 'modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs' (exists in tree). Task 5 creates 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs' (plausible, sibling to existing sbom.rs, advisory.rs) and modifies 'tests/Cargo.toml' (exists in tree). All paths align with the directory structure in repo-backend.md.

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map in impact-map.md lists 8 specific changes: 'Add AdvisorySeveritySummary model struct to represent severity counts (critical, high, medium, low, total)', 'Add service method to SbomService that queries the sbom_advisory join table, joins to advisory for severity, deduplicates by advisory ID, and aggregates counts by severity level', 'Add GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary endpoint with 5-minute cache configuration', 'Add optional ?threshold query parameter', 'Return 404 when SBOM ID does not exist', 'Add cache invalidation hook in advisory ingestion pipeline', 'Add integration tests...covering: success with counts, 404 for missing SBOM, threshold filtering, deduplication of advisories, cache behavior', 'Update API documentation'. All specify concrete technical details rather than vague descriptions.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks have a '## Repository' section containing only 'trustify-backend' — a single repository name. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in all task files.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Modified paths that exist exactly in repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs, tests/Cargo.toml. Created paths are new files within existing directories from the manifest: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (in the model/ dir alongside summary.rs, details.rs), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (in endpoints/ alongside get.rs, list.rs), tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (in tests/api/ alongside sbom.rs, advisory.rs). All paths follow the established structure.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 references: 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs' (SbomSummary), 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/details.rs' (SbomDetails), derive macros, serde patterns. Task 2 references: 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'entity/sbom_advisory.rs', 'entity/advisory.rs', Result<T, AppError> with .context(). Task 3 references: 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs', Axum Query extractor, tower-http caching middleware. Task 4 references: 'modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs', 'modules/ingestor/src/service/mod.rs', tower-http CacheLayer. Task 5 references: 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs', assert_eq!(resp.status(), StatusCode::OK) pattern. All are specific file paths and code patterns from the repository.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks have '## Target Branch' followed by 'main'. Task 1 line 7-8, Task 2 line 7-8, Task 3 line 7-8, Task 4 line 7-8, Task 5 line 7-8.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: Searched all output files for 'sha256', 'digest', and related terms. No matches found in any of the 6 output files (impact-map.md and 5 task files). There is no evidence that description digest comments were posted for any task.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: Searched all output files for 'convention', 'CONVENTIONS', 'applicab', and 'Applies:'. No convention applicability rationale was found in any task file. The only match was in Task 2's Implementation Notes referencing 'common/src/db/query.rs' which is a code reference, not a convention applicability rationale. There is no evidence of convention-aware enrichment with the prescribed 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>' format in any task.
